summary: Matched and linked the green shell item source by converting src/melee/it/items/itgshell.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The source changes are small but targeted: introduce temporaries to improve codegen around facing direction and random bounds, remove an unnecessary PAD_STACK, and factor duplicated hit-response logic into a static inline helper shellHit used by clank and shield-hit paths. Symbols for several local string/float/double constants were corrected from global it_* names to local @ labels in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itgshell.c is built as Object(Matching, ...) instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).
Adjusted it_8028B8D8 by introducing a f32 temp for -ip->facing_dir before multiplying v.x, likely to match compiler evaluation/codegen.
Adjusted random selection in it_8028BC2C and the shared hit path by assigning it_804D6D28->x48_byte to a u8 rand_max, masking it with 0xF, then passing it to HSD_Randi.
Factored duplicated green-shell hit response logic into static inline void shellHit(HSD_GObj* gobj).
Reused shellHit in itGShell_Logic14_Clanked and in the non-bounce branch of itGShell_Logic14_HitShield.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt so constants at 0x804D5270, 0x804D5278, 0x804DCA38, 0x804DCA3C, 0x804DCA40, and 0x804DCA48 are local @ labels rather than global it_* symbols.
smart_moves: Used small local temporaries rather than semantic rewrites to steer compiler output for matching.
Converted repeated clank/shield-hit behavior into a static inline helper, preserving inlined codegen while reducing duplicated source.
Kept the shield-hit control flow behavior intact: msid 3 or 4 still calls itColl_BounceOffVictim, while msid 5 through 8 uses the shellHit response.
Updated symbol scope alongside the source match so local constants are represented as local @ labels instead of misleading global it_* names.
Linked the object only after matching by moving itgshell.c to the Matching object list.
decomp_lessons: For item source matching, local variable extraction can be necessary even when the expression is equivalent, such as splitting -ip->facing_dir into a f32 temp before use.
For rand-mask patterns, assigning a byte field to a u8 local and masking the local can produce different codegen than passing a masked expression directly to HSD_Randi.
Static inline helpers can remove source duplication without forcing an out-of-line function, useful when two callbacks share identical compiled behavior.
PAD_STACK markers should be removed when no longer required for the final match; they are scaffolding, not semantic logic.
Matching a file may require symbol table cleanup for local constants, not just C source edits.
naming_conventions: New helper uses descriptive camel-case style shellHit rather than an address-based name.
Temporary random bound variable is named rand_max and typed u8, reflecting both purpose and storage width.
Local constants in symbols.txt use @number labels, while exported/global symbols keep it_* names.
Existing item callback names such as itGShell_Logic14_Clanked and itGShell_Logic14_HitShield were preserved.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduce a f32 temporary for unary negation before multiplication to influence floating-point codegen.
Use a u8 temporary for it_804D6D28->x48_byte, then apply rand_max &= 0xF before HSD_Randi(rand_max).
Replace duplicated code with a static inline helper to maintain inline callsite behavior while aligning shared logic.
Remove obsolete PAD_STACK once stack padding is no longer needed for the match.
Correct local/global symbol annotations for small data and small-data2 constants after matching exposes their true scope.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
There was one issue/comment text record counted, but no body excerpt was provided, so no substantive reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find other item files where duplicated clank and shield-hit logic can be factored into static inline helpers for matching.
Search for HSD_Randi calls using masked byte fields that may need u8 temporaries for matching.
Audit remaining global it_* constants in symbols.txt near item files to see whether any should be local @ labels.
Compare itgshell and itrshell matching tactics, especially shell collision and bounce logic.
Search for PAD_STACK in matched item files to identify removable stack-padding remnants.
